result_excerpt: # Result: Review this X thread: https://x.com/witcheer/status/2076717324585898343?s=43. Extract concrete Hermes skills, plugins, integrations, configuration or workflow recommendations. Evaluate each for a macOS autonomous assistant controlled primarily over Telegram, using OpenAI Codex gpt-5.6-terra with local terminal, files, browser, delegation and cron; it currently has no scheduled jobs. Deliver a short ranked shortlist: what it is, fit rationale, prerequisites/cost/risk, and an exact safe …
answer_summary:
**Top three recommendations:**

1. **Enable cron scheduling** — backbone for always-on operation. Draft an explicit allow-list of what unattended jobs may touch before deploying any entry. LOW-MEDIUM risk; zero infrastructure cost.

2. **Formalize maker-checker delegation** — second sub-task independently verifies before execution, critical for remote async Telegram control. Requires genuinely adversarial checker prompt (not just a rubber stamp). Draft a checker template for one high-risk action class (e.g., file deletes).

3. **Wire MCP servers for integrations** — OpenAI Codex CLI supports Model Context Protocol natively via `~/.codex/config.toml` (`mcp_servers` schema). Audit existing config and review `developers.openai.com/codex/config-reference` before adding new servers. MEDIUM risk per server (third-party code with execution privileges).
